opportunities.name: NWO Veni SSH
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained max 3 years ago
opportunities.deadline: Results current round mid-2026. Next round opens ~Sep 2026.
opportunities.tips: Pre-proposal then full proposal. Narrative CV describing 3 to 5 key outputs. Weighted 75% scientific quality, 25% impact. Cannot hire PhD candidates or postdocs from the grant.

opportunities.name: ERC Starting Grant 2027
opportunities.eligibility: 0 to 10 yrs post-PhD (widened from 2 to 7 starting 2027)
opportunities.tips: Panel SH1 (Individuals, Markets and Organisations: Economics, finance, management) covers HRM, OB, strategy, labour economics. Step 1 assesses vision and PI quality only (5 pages). Methodology assessed at Step 2 only. Must be genuinely groundbreaking.

opportunities.name: MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026
opportunities.eligibility: PhD holders with mobility rule: must not have lived in NL for more than 12 months in past 3 years
opportunities.tips: European or Global fellowship. Applicant and host supervisor write jointly. Requires a training and career development plan. Note: applications surged 65% in 2025, pushing success rates below 10%.

opportunities.name: KNAW Van der Gaag Grant
opportunities.eligibility: Behavioural or social science researcher, PhD obtained max 4 years ago
opportunities.tips: Small grant for conferences, short research visits, or pilot studies. Low administrative burden. Note: biennial cycle. Next application window expected September 2027.

opportunities.name: KNAW Early Career Partnerships
opportunities.eligibility: Early-career researchers at Dutch institutions
opportunities.tips: Funds interdisciplinary workshops and meetings. Useful for building cross-institutional collaborations before larger grant applications.

opportunities.name: NWO SSH Open Competition XS
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained at least 5 years ago (no upper limit since 2026)
opportunities.tips: Anonymous peer review by fellow applicants. Ideal for pilot studies, exploring new directions, or seed funding that strengthens a subsequent larger application.

opportunities.name: NWO Vidi SSH 2025
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained max 8 years ago
opportunities.tips: Can hire PhD candidates and postdocs. Pre-proposal, full proposal, then interview. Strategically crucial career grant. Coordinate with the dean well in advance given the embedding guarantee requirement.

opportunities.name: ERC Consolidator Grant 2027
opportunities.eligibility: 5 to 15 yrs post-PhD (widened from 7 to 12 starting 2027)
opportunities.tips: Panel SH1 (Economics, finance, management). Must demonstrate strong independent publication record and PhD supervision. The 2027 eligibility widening creates new opportunities for researchers previously caught between Starting and Consolidator windows.

opportunities.name: NWO SSH Open Competition M
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained at least 10 years ago
opportunities.tips: Funds one PhD or postdoc position alongside your own research time. Medium-scale independent programme. Alternates annually with the L scheme. Check NWO for call opening.

opportunities.name: NWO Vici SSH 2026
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained max 15 years ago
opportunities.tips: Funds an entire research group. Pre-proposal stage closed March 2026. Full proposal then interview. The highest-prestige NWO individual grant. Expect intense competition from across all SSH disciplines.

opportunities.name: ERC Advanced Grant 2026
opportunities.eligibility: Established research leaders, no career stage time limit
opportunities.tips: Panel SH1 (Economics, finance, management). Requires field-shaping track record and international recognition. The proposal should articulate a 10-year research vision. The additional equipment budget is €1M (or €2M if relocating from outside Europe). You do not need to be a full professor, but you must demonstrate exceptional scientific leadership.

opportunities.name: NWO SSH Open Competition L
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained at least 15 years ago
opportunities.tips: Largest NWO SSH individual grant. Funds a substantial research programme with multiple positions. Runs biennially. The 2025 round closed Feb 2026 and is now in assessment.

opportunities.name: ERC Proof of Concept
opportunities.eligibility: Must hold a current or recently completed ERC grant
opportunities.tips: Funds societal or commercial valorisation of ERC-funded research. Lump sum with light reporting. The highest success rate of any ERC scheme.

opportunities.name: KIC Human Capital & Digital Innovation
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium: researchers + SMEs + public stakeholders
opportunities.tips: HIGHLY RELEVANT. Focuses on labour productivity, strategic HRM in SMEs, and teams working with AI and robotics. Takes an integrated view of the innovation ecosystem, organisational HRM policy, and individual/team adaptation to technology. Start identifying industry partners now.

opportunities.name: NWO Gravitation (Zwaartekracht) 7th Round
opportunities.eligibility: World-class research consortium
opportunities.tips: Budget €161M for approximately 6 to 7 consortia. Requires absolute world-leading position. SSH-oriented Gravitation projects exist. Extremely competitive but transformative if successful.

opportunities.name: KIEM Arbeidsbesparende AI (Labor-saving AI)
opportunities.eligibility: Min 1 knowledge institution + 1 SME or public org
opportunities.tips: Small exploratory grants for testing existing AI applications in organisations. Focus on labour savings and productivity gains. Practice-oriented. Excellent seed funding to prepare a larger KIC Human Capital bid.

opportunities.name: KIEM MV (Social Earning Capacity)
opportunities.eligibility: Network of knowledge institutions + practice partners
opportunities.deadline: Two collection dates: 24 Mar and 15 Sep 2026
opportunities.tips: Exploratory applied research under the KIA Social Earning Capacity. Broad scope covering HRM, organisational innovation, and human capital development. Low barrier seed funding.

opportunities.name: COST Actions 2026 Open Call
opportunities.eligibility: Pan-European researcher network (proposer + min 7 countries)
opportunities.tips: Funds workshops, training schools, short-term scientific missions. Not research itself. COST participants show a 39% success rate in subsequent Horizon Europe applications. Strategic precursor to larger bids.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 4: Human/AI Collaboration for Workforce
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium
opportunities.tips: AI-based human-machine interaction, inclusion, well-being, upskilling, and career evolution. Two-stage call opening Sep 2026. Directly relevant for OB researchers studying technology and work.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: Private Companies & Democracy
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium
opportunities.tips: Corporate governance, CSR, and democratic engagement. Part of the Cluster 2 2027 work programme.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: EU Labour Mobility Impact
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium
opportunities.tips: Worker mobility, migration, and labour market integration across the EU.

opportunities.name: NWO Veni SSH 2026 Round
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained max 3 years ago
opportunities.tips: Annual cycle. Watch NWO SSH page for exact dates.

opportunities.name: NWO SSH Open Competition M (next round)
opportunities.eligibility: PhD obtained at least 10 years ago
opportunities.tips: Alternates with L round. Monitor NWO announcements.

opportunities.name: ZonMw Work & Health programmes
opportunities.eligibility: Researchers at the health/work intersection
opportunities.tips: Relevant when framing OB research around occupational health, burnout prevention, sustainable employability, or healthcare workforce issues.

opportunities.name: KNAW Humanities Fund
opportunities.eligibility: SSH researchers at Dutch institutions
opportunities.tips: Funds research visits and small events. Very low barrier.

opportunities.name: Wellcome Awards (Early-Career Awards, Career Development Awards, Discovery Awards, Mental Health Award)
opportunities.eligibility: Different per award
opportunities.tips: This is a UK-based fund, it seams more promising to collaborate with UK-based colleagues.

opportunities.name: Volkswagen Stiftung (Societal Transformations, Exploration, Understanding Research)
opportunities.eligibility: Different per award
opportunities.tips: This is a German-based fund, it seams more promising to collaborate with colleagues form Germany.

opportunities.name: NWA-ORC 'Reinventing Work'
opportunities.eligibility: Transdisciplinary consortium with academic and societal partners
opportunities.tips: Must span the entire knowledge chain: fundamental, applied, and practice-oriented research. The 'Reinventing Work' theme covers digital labour, platform work, autonomy versus control, and solidarity. Mandatory collaborative workshops ran in Feb/Mar 2026. Societal partners required.

opportunities.name: KIC Demand-driven Consortia S 2026
opportunities.eligibility: Min 2 research organisations + 1 company. Co-funding min 30% (half private).
opportunities.tips: Thematically free as long as it aligns with a KIA. The S variant is designed for smaller consortia that want to grow into sustained partnerships. Information meeting 14 April 2026. Good entry point for building industry collaborations around HRM/OB topics.

opportunities.name: KIC Demand-driven Consortia XL
opportunities.eligibility: Min 2 research organisations + 1 company. Co-funding min 30% (half private cash).
opportunities.tips: Thematically free. Large-scale public-private partnerships. Pre-proposal deadline passed (10 Mar 2026). You can still join an existing consortium as co-applicant or wait for the next round.

opportunities.name: KIC Adaptivity in Transitions
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium with co-funders. Mandatory workshop attendance for lead applicant.
opportunities.tips: Focused on food, mobility, and energy transitions, but the core question is about adaptivity of organisations and individuals in change processes. OB researchers can contribute on organisational learning, change management, and human capital.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: Productivity
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium of min 3 entities from min 3 countries
opportunities.tips: Topic: Strengthen Europe's social model through productivity. Research and Innovation Action with 100% funding plus 25% flat rate for indirect costs.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: Talent Magnet
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium of min 3 entities from min 3 countries
opportunities.tips: Making Europe a global magnet for talent. Covers attracting and retaining researchers and high-skilled workers. Directly relevant to talent management and international HRM research.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: Skills & Green Transition
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium of min 3 entities from min 3 countries
opportunities.tips: Fostering competencies for the green transition. Covers workforce development, skills anticipation, and training system design.

opportunities.name: HE Cluster 2: Government in Transition
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium of min 3 entities from min 3 countries
opportunities.tips: How governments prepare the civil service for the future. Strong public sector management and HR angle. Workforce planning, digital skills in government, organisational transformation.

opportunities.name: EU Partnership: Social Transformations
opportunities.eligibility: Institutional and national partnership
opportunities.tips: Seven-year co-funded partnership covering future of work, social protection, education and skills, and just transitions. Co-funded by national agencies including NWO. Positioning yourself early creates a sustained funding pipeline through 2034.

opportunities.name: MSCA Doctoral Networks 2026
opportunities.eligibility: Consortium of academic and non-academic partners across countries
opportunities.tips: Structured PhD training programme with strong intersectoral and international mobility requirements. Each doctoral candidate must spend time at a non-academic partner.

opportunities.name: MSCA Staff Exchanges 2026
opportunities.eligibility: Academic and non-academic partners, international
opportunities.tips: Funds short-term staff secondments between partners for knowledge transfer. Relatively high success rate. Good networking instrument that can lead to larger collaborative bids.

opportunities.name: ERC Synergy Grant 2027
opportunities.eligibility: 2 to 4 PIs at any career stage with complementary expertise
opportunities.tips: Funds ambitious research that could not be achieved by any single PI alone. Requires genuine intellectual synergy, not just a sum of parts. Cross-disciplinary combinations encouraged.

tips.detail: NWO uses the Evidence-Based CV format. You describe 3 to 5 key outputs in narrative form, explaining what you uniquely contributed and why it mattered. There is no h-index, no journal impact factor, and no publication count. The story you tell about your trajectory matters more than metrics. Choose outputs that demonstrate independence, leadership, and impact on the field.

This is where many strong proposals lose marks. NWO expects concrete stakeholders named by organisation, specific dissemination channels (not just 'we will publish papers'), and a realistic timeline. For HRM/OB research, think about HR professionals, employer organisations like AWVN or VNO-NCW, trade unions, policy bodies like SER or SCP, and practitioner outlets like CHRO or PW. Generic statements like 'we will share findings with practitioners' will not score well.

SSH has six disciplinary panels. Organisational psychology and work psychology proposals typically go to the 'Behaviour and Education' panel. Business administration goes to a separate panel. If your research sits between panels (e.g., combining OB theory with economic methodology), contact NWO in advance. Being assessed by the wrong panel can be fatal because reviewers may not appreciate discipline-specific contributions.

The most frequent reasons for rejection are: insufficient innovation (the project advances the field incrementally rather than opening a new direction), vague or underspecified methodology (especially for ESM and diary studies where power analysis and sampling rationale are critical), weak impact plans, and inaccessible writing. Remember that NWO panels are interdisciplinary. An economist and a psychologist will both read your proposal. Minimise jargon and explain why your approach is novel to someone outside your subfield.

At our faculty, any externally funded project that involves hiring new personnel or changing education load percentages requires an embedding guarantee. This applies to all grants listed in this document, not just NWO Vidi. If unsure whether your project triggers this requirement, ask the research director. Coordinate with the dean well in advance because it is a formal institutional commitment that takes time to arrange.

NWO allows resubmission of revised proposals. If you are rejected, request the full reviewer reports and panel discussion summary. The most successful resubmissions address every specific criticism rather than simply rewriting the introduction. If the panel questioned your methodology, your revision must demonstrate that you heard and responded to that concern.

SH1 (Individuals, Markets and Organisations: Economics, finance, management) covers labour economics, HRM, organisation studies, strategy, entrepreneurship, management, and behavioural economics. SH1 has its own dedicated budget, so you compete only against other social scientists. Depending on your angle, SH2 (Institutions, Governance), SH3 (The Social World), or SH4 (The Human Mind) may also fit. Read the panel descriptors carefully and choose strategically.

tips.topic: What 'groundbreaking' means in the social sciences
tips.detail: ERC expects proposals that challenge existing paradigms, introduce genuinely novel theoretical frameworks, or develop new methodologies. Applying an existing theory to a new empirical context is not sufficient. You need to articulate what is fundamentally wrong or missing in current understanding and how your project will change the conversation. Think of it as a 5-year programme that redefines how the field thinks about a problem.

The proposal now separates the scientific vision (Part I, 5 pages, assessed at Step 1) from methodology and implementation (Part II, 7 pages, assessed only at Step 2). This means Step 1 evaluators judge the research vision and the PI track record (CV is assessed at Step 1 too). Feasibility comes later. Lead with ambition and vision in Part I. Do not waste space on methodological detail that will only be read at Step 2.

tips.topic: Track record expectations by career stage
tips.detail: Starting Grant: evidence of independence from your PhD supervisor, first steps building your own research line, and early signs of international recognition (invited talks, editorial board membership, small grants). Consolidator Grant: strong independent publication record in top journals, successful PhD supervision, evidence of international collaboration, and a clear emerging reputation. Advanced Grant: field-shaping contributions that have changed how other researchers think, major awards or recognitions, and sustained international leadership.

Starting from 2027, the Starting Grant window widens to 0 to 10 years post-PhD (previously 2 to 7) and the Consolidator window to 5 to 15 years (previously 7 to 12). Identify department members who were previously ineligible because they fell between windows. Someone 9 years post-PhD was too late for Starting and too early for Consolidator under the old rules. They can now apply for either.

The Netherlands consistently ranks among the top ERC host countries. In 2024-2025, Dutch institutions received over 40 Starting Grants per year. Evaluators are familiar with Dutch universities as strong research environments. RUG's track record as a host institution is an asset.

tips.topic: Impact section: where most proposals fail
tips.detail: Evaluators report that the impact section is the weakest part of most proposals. Be extremely specific. Instead of 'our findings will inform policy', write 'we will deliver a policy brief to DG EMPL by month 24 and present findings at the OECD Future of Work Forum in year 3'. Name stakeholder organisations. Define measurable KPIs for dissemination. Letters of support from stakeholders help.

Minimum 3 independent entities from 3 countries (at least 1 EU Member State). Successful Cluster 2 projects typically involve 8 to 15 partners. Every partner must have a clearly justified role with adequate resources. Evaluators penalise 'flag planting' where partners are included for geographical coverage without substantive tasks.

tips.topic: Funding rate and real cost to the university
tips.detail: Research and Innovation Actions receive 100% direct cost funding plus a 25% flat rate for indirect costs. The university bears essentially zero cost for participating. This makes consortium participation attractive even in smaller roles. Budget personnel costs realistically using standard Dutch salary scales.

Start 12 months before the deadline. Use the Net4SocietyHE Community Platform to find partners. Attend brokerage events (the next major one is planned for late May/June 2026). COST Action membership is one of the best consortium pipelines: build a 4-year network through COST, then convert it into a Horizon Europe consortium.

Budget 48 hours before the deadline for submission. The EU Funding & Tenders Portal routinely overloads in the final hours. Have all partner data ready weeks in advance: PIC numbers, legal entity validation documents, and signed consortium agreements.

tips.topic: What is KIC and why should we care?
tips.detail: The Knowledge and Innovation Covenant is NWO's largest programmatic investment: ~€138M per year in public-private research consortia (€120M for Mission/Demand/Strategy lines plus €18M for Practice) addressing societal challenges. All KIC research must align with one of 8 Knowledge and Innovation Agendas. For HRM/OB, the most relevant KIAs are Social Earning Capacity, Digitalisation, and Health & Care.

NWO funds 50 to 80% depending on the scheme. The remainder must come from co-funders, and at least one company must participate. Co-funding can be partly in-kind (staff time, data access, facilities). Building industry partnerships is essential. Start conversations with potential partners 6 to 12 months before a call opens.

NWO organises matchmaking events for each KIC call. For some calls (e.g., Adaptivity in Transitions), attendance is mandatory for lead applicants. Even when not mandatory, these events are where consortia form and where you meet the industry partners needed for co-funding.

KIEM grants (€40K, 1 year) are explicitly designed as seeds for larger KIC bids. They fund exploratory practice-oriented research with at least one industry partner. Use KIEM to establish a working relationship with an SME, generate pilot data, and build the case for a €1-7M KIC Demand-driven Consortia proposal.

tips.topic: KIC Human Capital call: prepare now
tips.detail: The KIC Human Capital and Digital Innovation call is in preparation and directly targets strategic HRM in SMEs, teams working with AI and robotics, and innovation ecosystems. This is the most relevant upcoming KIC call for our department. Start identifying SME partners who are implementing AI. When the call opens, you want a consortium and a research question already in place.

tips.topic: Build consortium readiness before calls open
tips.detail: The most common reason for missing consortium opportunities is starting too late. Use COST Actions, Net4SocietyHE, and NWO matchmaking events to build partnerships continuously. When a call opens, the department should be able to assemble a consortium within weeks, not months.

tips.topic: Ensure every eligible researcher applies to NWO
tips.detail: The NWO Talent Scheme remains the backbone of academic career funding in the Netherlands. Every eligible researcher should be actively preparing for the appropriate tier. The department should offer internal mock reviews: having 3 to 4 colleagues read and critique a proposal before submission dramatically improves success rates. Schedule mock reviews at least 6 weeks before the deadline.

HRM/OB research on topics like multiple team membership, virtual teaming, or identity processes becomes fundable at a completely different scale when framed around societal challenges: future of work, AI and work, sustainable employability, mental health at work, or the green transition workforce. This is not about changing your research. It is about articulating why your research matters to society in language that interdisciplinary panels and policy makers understand.

The University of Groningen has dedicated faculty funding officers and a Talent Development editorial team that provides proposal review for NWO and ERC applications. Book them 3 to 6 months before your deadline. For Horizon Europe, the central EU liaison office can assist with budget calculations, PIC numbers, and portal submission.

Winning researchers prepare 6 to 12 months before deadlines, not 6 to 12 weeks. For ERC: start 9 months out. For Horizon Europe consortia: start 12 months out. For NWO Talent Scheme: start 6 months out. The final month should be for polish and administrative preparation, not developing the core idea.
